summary.intent: Fix the broken plugin installation story for the optimize-anything repo by implementing a proper marketplace-based distribution mechanism and correct CI workflow triggers.
summary.outcome: Created a marketplace.json catalog file, corrected installation documentation in README.md and install.md to use real Claude Code plugin commands, and fixed CI workflow to trigger on all PRs while restricting the integration job to pushes to main only.

repo: optimize-anything lives at ASRagab/optimize-anything on GitHub
Plugin structure uses .claude-plugin/ directory with plugin.json manifest, plus top-level commands/ and skills/ directories
CI uses GitHub Actions at .github/workflows/ci.yml with separate test and integration jobs; integration job consumes OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini API keys
marketplace.json uses 'source': './' to allow a single repo to serve as both marketplace catalog and plugin source

code.finding: Newly created marketplace catalog file; uses 'source': './' pattern so the repo root serves as both marketplace and plugin. Plugin ID format for install command is 'optimize-anything@optimize-anything' (plugin-name@marketplace-name).
Final trigger: pull_request with no branch filter (fires on all PRs) + push to main. Integration job gated by: if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'

workflow: Claude Code plugin installation is a two-step marketplace model: '/plugin marketplace add <owner/repo>' then '/plugin install <plugin>@<marketplace>'. The command 'claude plugin add <url>' does not exist.
A repo must have a .claude-plugin/marketplace.json to be installable as a Claude Code plugin, even if the repo contains only one plugin.

GitHub Actions PR triggers default to only PRs targeting a filtered branch if branches filter is specified; omitting the branches filter on pull_request fires on PRs from any source branch to any target branch.

friction: Initial CI edit only added the integration job condition but did not update the 'on:' trigger — required a second pass after the user pointed out the oversight.
claude plugin add <url> was a nonexistent command documented in the repo, requiring research to determine the actual Claude Code plugin distribution model before any fixes could be made.
open_items: Marketplace.json and plugin structure have not been validated by actually running '/plugin marketplace add ASRagab/optimize-anything' in a live Claude Code session — end-to-end install flow is untested.
The repo may benefit from a GitHub Actions job that validates marketplace.json schema on every PR to prevent future documentation drift.
No uninstall workflow was added to the CI; if the plugin install/uninstall commands change in future Claude Code versions, install.md will need updating again.
